qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated migrate endpoint allows anonymous database migration execution and site-wide denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets AppController::actionMigrate anonymous access and migration execution, while the advisory root cause is unsandboxed Twig rendering in BaseElementSelectConditionRule::getElementIds() for relational conditionals.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: User account enumeration via differentiated login lockout error messages
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes login error differentiation and lockout state disclosure, not the Craft CMS conditions system path that passes relation input to renderObjectTemplate().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described Craft CMS condition-rule RCE root cause in BaseElementSelectConditionRule::getElementIds(), where user-controlled relational condition input is rendered through unsandboxed Twig via renderObjectTemplate().
evidence: The advisory describes RCE in Craft CMS 4.x/5.x conditions: BaseElementSelectConditionRule::getElementIds() passes user-controlled strings into renderObjectTemplate() with unsandboxed Twig rendering.
The unauthenticated_migrate_endpoint finding describes anonymous access to AppController::actionMigrate and pending migration execution, not condition-rule template rendering.
The user-enumeration-login finding describes differentiated login lockout errors, not template injection or condition processing.
A session search found no qualifying finding references to BaseElementSelectConditionRule, getElementIds, renderObjectTemplate, or the advisory condition-rule control flow.
